description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7.5 agentic cluster: live A2A/MCP message security (ASI07) vs agentic supply chain (ASI04), external API/webhook contracts, in-context injection, and the identity model.

cases.prompt: Review whether our agents' messages to each other and to our MCP servers can be spoofed, tampered with, or replayed.
cases.reason: Spoofing, tampering, and replay of live agent/MCP messages is ASI07; the supply-chain reviewer owns install-time package trust and api-event-architect owns external partner contracts.

cases.prompt: Vet the MCP servers and plugins in our manifest before we install them — registries, maintainers, pinning, what their manifests request.
cases.reason: Acquisition trust of MCP servers, manifests, registries, and plugin packages is the ASI04 agentic supply chain; ASI07 begins once installed components exchange live messages.

cases.prompt: Design signed, tenant-scoped webhooks with retries and replay protection for our partner integrations.
cases.reason: External integration contracts (webhooks, event feeds, versioned envelopes) are api-event-architect; ASI07 covers the internal agent mesh and MCP transports.

cases.prompt: Hidden instructions inside a document our agent read made it change behavior — build the defense for untrusted content in context.
cases.reason: Content already in the model context is LLM01 injection defense; the comms reviewer secures the transport and receiver handling of peer messages before/as they arrive.

cases.prompt: Decide what identities our agents should hold and how a user's authority delegates through sub-agents.
cases.reason: The identity and delegation architecture is ASI03; the comms reviewer consumes those identities to evaluate per-edge authentication.

cases.prompt: If one agent in our pipeline goes bad, its outputs cascade into every downstream agent — design the isolation and circuit breakers.
cases.reason: Fault propagation, blast-radius isolation, and circuit breakers across the agent network are the ASI08 half of containment; the comms reviewer secures individual message exchanges, not failure spread.
